where_to_eat.name: Mercado Juarez
where_to_eat.description: A legendary food market that food enthusiasts get lost in and refuse to leave. Known for authentic Mexican cuisine that spoils visitors from eating tacos anywhere else.

into_the_wild.name: Teotihuacán
into_the_wild.description: Ancient pre-Columbian pyramids outside Mexico City, most famous for the Pyramid of the Sun which visitors could once climb for panoramic views.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Casa Azul
gram_worthy_spots.description: Frida Kahlo's iconic blue house museum in Coyoacán, a culturally significant and visually stunning destination that showcases feminist art and history.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Centro Histórico
gram_worthy_spots.description: Mexico City's historic center with an international feel, historic architecture, and cultural significance that attracts photographers and travelers alike.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Coyoacán
gram_worthy_spots.description: A charming, artsy neighborhood popular with tourists and photographers, known for its bohemian atmosphere and cultural landmarks.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Local street art in Centro
off_the_beaten_path.description: Hidden street art and graffiti throughout the city center featuring artistic messages and political commentary reflecting Mexico City's vibrant urban culture.
